message:
feat(pager,wal): fix savepoint rollback page leaks, add cache eviction, and optimize WAL I/O

This commit addresses several correctness and performance issues in the pager
and WAL subsystems.

**Savepoint rollback reclaims allocated pages**: Previously, rolling back a
savepoint did not undo page allocations made after the savepoint was created.
This caused page number "holes" -- pages allocated inside a savepoint that were
never returned to the freelist on rollback, permanently leaking space.
`SavepointEntry` now snapshots `next_page`, `freelist`, and a new
`allocated_from_freelist` tracker. On `rollback_to_savepoint()`, these are
restored, ensuring page numbers and freelist state are fully reverted. The
`savepoint()` method now acquires the inner lock to capture consistent state.

**Transaction rollback reclaims freelist pages**: `SimpleTransaction` now tracks
`allocated_from_freelist: Vec<PageNumber>`. When `allocate_page()` pops from the
freelist, the page is recorded. On `rollback()`, all such pages are pushed back
onto the freelist, preventing leaks when a transaction allocates a freed page
and then aborts.

**Page cache eviction under memory pressure**: `PageCache` gains `evict_any()`
which removes an arbitrary page to free a buffer. `PagerInner::read_page()` now
retries buffer pool acquisition after evicting a cache entry on `OutOfMemory`,
enabling the pager to serve reads even when the buffer pool is fully committed
to cached pages. `write_page()` similarly attempts eviction before giving up on
caching. A new test (`test_cache_eviction_under_pressure`) verifies correct
behavior with 300 pages against a 256-page cache.

**S3-FIFO self-healing**: `evict_small()` and `evict_main()` previously
returned early (skipping eviction) when a page was found in the FIFO queue
but missing from the index -- an invariant violation indicating a bug. Now
they self-heal by treating the entry as unaccessed and proceeding with normal
eviction logic, preventing the cache from getting stuck.

**WAL `read_frame_into` zero-allocation API**: A new `read_frame_into(cx,
frame_index, buf)` method reads a frame into a caller-provided buffer,
eliminating per-frame `Vec` allocation. `read_frame()` is refactored to
delegate to `read_frame_into()`. The checkpoint executor now reuses a single
frame buffer across all frames instead of allocating one per iteration.

**WAL checksum unrolling**: The `sqlite_wal_checksum` inner loop previously
processed both u32 words in a single accumulation step. This was incorrect
per the SQLite WAL spec -- each word should independently accumulate s1 and
s2. The loop is now unrolled to process x0 and x1 separately, matching the
reference implementation's behavior.

**Frame offset overflow safety**: `WalFile::frame_offset()` and the frame
scanning loop in `open()` now use explicit u64 intermediates with comments
explaining why, preventing usize overflow on 32-bit targets for large WAL
files. A test verifies the arithmetic with an index (1,042,468) that would
overflow 32-bit multiplication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
